data.title: BitMart launches an AMM Bot market-making mechanism, allowing users to share transaction fee revenue.
data.description: According to official news, BitMart has launched a new automated market-making tool, AMM Bot, introducing automated liquidity solutions for select spot trading pairs. Based on a constant product market-making algorithm, this feature automatically places buy and sell orders across multiple price ranges through independent liquidity pools, maintaining stable trading depth for trading pairs even without manual order placement. Users simply need to deposit two assets into the pool simultaneously to participate in market making and become liquidity providers. In terms of rewards, AMM Bot provides users with the opportunity to continuously earn trading fee dividends. Transaction fees generated by the relevant trading pairs will be distributed to liquidity providers according to their liquidity contribution, allowing market-making users to share in the profits generated by market activity. Furthermore, AMM Bot supports flexible increases and decreases in liquidity, providing users with a more efficient way to utilize their funds.

data.title: Ava Labs Chief Strategy Officer Steps Down After Careful Consideration
data.description: According to Foresight News, Luigi D'Onorio DeMeo, the Chief Strategy Officer of Ava Labs, announced his resignation from the position. He stated that the decision was made after extensive discussions and reflection. Over the past few months, DeMeo has been formally transitioning his responsibilities and preparing for a new chapter in his life.

data.title: Newrez, a major US lending institution, plans to include crypto assets in its mortgage lending assessment, eliminating the need for upfront liquidation.
data.description: Newrez, a major U.S. mortgage lender, announced plans to roll out a new policy in the U.S. starting in February, allowing borrowers to include their crypto assets in their mortgage application assessment without having to convert those assets into fiat currency beforehand. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: PancakeSwap: Plans to reduce maximum cake supply from 450 million to 400 million.
data.description: PancakeSwap announced on its X platform that voting on the proposal to reduce the maximum supply of CAKE tokens is now officially open. The proposal aims to reduce the maximum supply of CAKE tokens from 450 million to 400 million. Community members can now vote on this official proposal.

data.title: Iranian protests saw massive Bitcoin withdrawals, and the rial's sharp drop fueled safe-haven demand.
data.description: Amid anti-government protests and government internet censorship in multiple parts of Iran, local residents are accelerating the transfer of Bitcoin to personal wallets. Chainalysis, a blockchain analytics firm, points out a significant increase in BTC withdrawals from local Iranian exchanges to unknown personal wallets between December 28, 2024, the start of the protests, and January 8, when the internet outage occurred. This indicates that people are holding their Bitcoin in custody more frequently during periods of social unrest. Chainalysis believes this behavior is closely related to the sharp depreciation of the Iranian rial. Data shows that the rial's exchange rate against the US dollar plummeted from approximately 420,000 to over 1.05 million in a short period, resulting in a rapid loss of purchasing power. In this context, Bitcoin, with its decentralized, censorship-resistant, and cross-border transfer characteristics, is seen as an important tool to hedge against currency collapse and economic instability. The report also notes that this trend is consistent with other regions globally experiencing war, economic crises, or repressive government policies. Furthermore, Chainalysis also revealed that addresses associated with the Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) accounted for over 50% of the total amount of crypto assets received in Iran in the fourth quarter of 2025, with on-chain processing exceeding $3 billion for the entire year. (CoinDesk)

data.title: OKX launches a team-based coin-earning event; team up to complete tasks and receive a 200U reward.
data.description: According to official news, OKX's "Collective Earning for Contracts Season" event is now officially launched. During the event, users can participate by forming teams of 5 new OKX users. Once the entire team completes registration, KYC verification, and contract grid trading tasks, they can receive a 200 USDT real-time reward. The reward is exclusively awarded to the team leader, while the invited contributors will receive exclusive rewards for new users. This event is for a limited time only, with no limit to the number of times a team can be formed. Each successful team formation and completion of a task will earn a 200 USDT reward, which can be accumulated multiple times. Users can access the event page through the event card on the OKX App homepage to participate in team formation and win rewards.

data.title: Bitget has launched USD-based FRAX and FOGO perpetual contracts.
data.description: According to an official announcement, Bitget has launched USDT-based FRAX and FOGO perpetual contracts, with maximum leverage ratios of 50x and 25x respectively. A contract trading bot will also be available simultaneously. Additionally, participants can join the FOGO CandyBomb event to unlock airdrop rewards by completing specific contract trading volume tasks. Each participant can receive up to 5,000 FOGOs, with a total prize pool of 1,000,000 FOGOs. The event ends on January 22nd at 22:00 (UTC+8).

data.title: Belgium's KBC Group to Launch Bitcoin and Ethereum Trading Services
data.description:
According to PANews, Belgium's second-largest bank, KBC Group, has announced plans to offer Bitcoin and Ethereum trading services to retail customers through its online investment platform, Bolero. This initiative makes KBC the first bank in the country to provide such services. The launch is scheduled for the week of February 16 and will operate under the regulatory framework of the EU's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA).

KBC has submitted a comprehensive notification to regulatory authorities regarding its crypto asset service offerings. The service will follow an 'execution-only' model, requiring customers to make their own decisions and complete a risk knowledge and experience test before trading. To mitigate fraud and money laundering risks, the platform will use a 'closed-loop' model, allowing customers to buy and sell only within the Bolero platform. Assets cannot be transferred to external wallets or exchanges, and the bank will provide custody services.

data.title: US Democratic lawmakers urged the SEC to explain the reasons for suspending the enforcement case against Justin Sun.
data.description: Three Democratic members of the U.S. House of Representatives sent a letter to Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chairman Paul Atkins, requesting an explanation regarding the SEC's suspension of enforcement proceedings against TRON founder Justin Sun. Representatives Maxine Waters, Brad Sherman, and Sean Casten stated that the prolonged delay in the case could undermine investor confidence in the regulator's independence and credibility. The letter noted that the SEC has recently initiated or is pursuing suspensions or withdrawals of several crypto-related enforcement cases, including those involving Binance, Coinbase, and Kraken, and questioned the focus and effectiveness of its enforcement efforts. Regarding the Sun case, the SEC requested a court stay of proceedings in February to explore potential solutions, a process that has lasted approximately 11 months. The representatives also mentioned Sun's token transactions with World Liberty Financial, requesting the SEC to retain and provide all documents and communications related to the suspension of the case. The SEC has not yet publicly responded to these inquiries. (Cointelegraph)
